title: Acute Coronary Syndrome After Yard Work

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
58-year-old client with hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports crushing substernal chest pressure that began while raking leaves and is not relieved by rest.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• chest pressure radiating to left jaw
• diaphoresis with nausea
• ST depression in leads II, III, aVF
• pain rated 2/10 after nitroglycerin
• oriented and able to describe symptoms

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical telemetry unit
72-year-old client with heart failure and chronic kidney disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Admitted for shortness of breath after missing two doses of furosemide.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• SpO2 86% on room air
• pink frothy sputum with crackles
• new S3 heart sound and orthopnea
• trace ankle edema unchanged from baseline
• temperature 98.6 F

title: Atrial Fibrillation With Rapid Ventricular Response

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Telemetry unit
81-year-old client with history of hypertension.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports palpitations and lightheadedness beginning 2 hours ago.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• irregularly irregular rhythm at 148/min
• blood pressure 88/54 mm Hg
• new dizziness and cool clammy skin
• oxygen saturation 96% on 2 L nasal cannula
• urine output 35 mL/hr

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Clinic/triage
76-year-old client taking digoxin and furosemide.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Calls the clinic reporting nausea, poor appetite, and seeing yellow halos.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• apical pulse 48/min
• potassium 3.1 mEq/L
• yellow-green visual halos
• mild ankle edema at baseline
• blood pressure 128/72 mm Hg

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
64-year-old client with uncontrolled hypertension.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Arrives with severe headache, blurred vision, and chest tightness.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood pressure 226/124 mm Hg
• new confusion
• creatinine elevated from baseline
• reports taking multivitamin
• temperature 98.8 F

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
79-year-old client from home with indwelling urinary catheter.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Family reports fever, weakness, and new confusion over 24 hours.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• temperature 102.7 F
• blood pressure 82/48 mm Hg
• lactate 4.2 mmol/L
• reports chronic knee pain
• hearing aids at bedside

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
67-year-old client admitted with community-acquired pneumonia.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Now reports increased shortness of breath and chills.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• SpO2 88% on 4 L nasal cannula
• respiratory rate 32/min with accessory muscle use
• coarse crackles over right lower lobe
• productive cough with prescribed sputum specimen pending
• appetite reduced

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
69-year-old client with severe COPD.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Arrives with worsening dyspnea after several days of increased sputum.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• drowsiness and headache
• PaCO2 68 mm Hg
• pH 7.29 with wheezes
• barrel chest present for years
• uses pursed-lip breathing

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Urgent care
24-year-old client with asthma.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports chest tightness after exposure to a cat and has used albuterol six times today.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• speaks in one-word phrases
• silent chest areas on auscultation
• SpO2 89% on room air
• heart rate 112/min after albuterol
• mild anxiety

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Postoperative orthopedic unit
54-year-old client 2 days after knee arthroplasty.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Suddenly reports sharp chest pain and shortness of breath when getting out of bed.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• sudden pleuritic chest pain
• SpO2 87% on room air
• heart rate 126/min with unilateral calf swelling
• incision dressing dry
• reports expected knee soreness

title: Tension Pneumothorax After Central Line Attempt

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: ICU step-down
48-year-old client after attempted subclavian central line insertion.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops sudden respiratory distress 10 minutes after the procedure.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• tracheal deviation away from affected side
• absent breath sounds on left
• hypotension with distended neck veins
• dry dressing over insertion site
• client is anxious

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Surgical unit
62-year-old client 18 hours after abdominal surgery.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has low-grade fever and reports shallow breathing because of incisional pain.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• diminished bases
• SpO2 91% on room air
• poor incentive spirometer effort due to pain
• incision edges approximated
• bowel sounds hypoactive

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
61-year-old client taking ibuprofen daily for arthritis.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports vomiting coffee-ground material and feeling faint.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• coffee-ground emesis
• blood pressure 86/50 mm Hg
• hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL
• last bowel movement yesterday
• mild epigastric burning for months

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
45-year-old client with history of alcohol use disorder.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports severe epigastric pain radiating to the back after a binge episode.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• severe epigastric pain radiating to back
• lipase markedly elevated
• rigid abdomen with vomiting
• reports last meal was spicy
• skin intact

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
38-year-old client who is postpartum 6 weeks.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports right upper quadrant pain after eating fried food.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• right upper quadrant pain radiating to right shoulder
• positive Murphy sign
• fever with leukocytosis
• normal bowel sounds
• mild nausea

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
19-year-old client with abdominal pain.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Pain began near umbilicus and migrated to right lower quadrant.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• right lower quadrant rebound tenderness
• fever 101.8 F
• white blood cells 16,000/mm3
• reports anorexia
• last menstrual period 2 weeks ago

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
70-year-old client with prior abdominal surgeries.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports cramping abdominal pain, vomiting, and no flatus for 24 hours.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• high-pitched bowel sounds
• abdominal distention
• persistent vomiting with metabolic alkalosis
• surgical scar healed
• reports thirst

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
52-year-old client with untreated peptic ulcer disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops sudden severe abdominal pain and arrives pale and guarding.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• sudden knife-like epigastric pain
• rigid board-like abdomen
• free air under diaphragm on x-ray
• reports chronic heartburn
• last antacid taken yesterday

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
22-year-old client with type 1 diabetes.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports vomiting and missed insulin after viral illness.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood glucose 486 mg/dL
• Kussmaul respirations with fruity breath
• pH 7.21 and positive serum ketones
• dry mucous membranes
• abdominal pain

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
78-year-old client with type 2 diabetes.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Family reports progressive confusion and extreme thirst after pneumonia.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood glucose 835 mg/dL
• serum osmolality elevated
• profound dehydration without significant ketones
• respiratory rate 18/min
• reports dry mouth

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
65-year-old client with type 2 diabetes receiving insulin.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Becomes shaky and diaphoretic 2 hours after breakfast.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood glucose 48 mg/dL
• diaphoresis and tremors
• new confusion
• skin intact
• blood pressure 138/76 mm Hg

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
59-year-old client with small cell lung cancer.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops headache, nausea, and increasing confusion.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• serum sodium 118 mEq/L
• low serum osmolality
• concentrated urine with decreased urine output
• blood glucose 102 mg/dL
• skin warm and dry

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Neuro ICU
33-year-old client after traumatic brain injury.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has sudden high urine output and intense thirst.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• urine output 600 mL/hr
• serum sodium 154 mEq/L
• urine specific gravity 1.002
• temperature 99.1 F
• pupils unchanged

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
70-year-old client taking thiazide diuretic.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports fatigue and nausea; spouse says the client is more confused.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• serum sodium 120 mEq/L
• new confusion
• muscle cramps and headache
• blood glucose 96 mg/dL
• oxygen saturation 97%

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
63-year-old client with chronic kidney disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Missed dialysis and reports weakness and palpitations.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• potassium 6.8 mEq/L
• peaked T waves on ECG
• muscle weakness
• blood pressure 150/82 mm Hg
• mild ankle edema

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Telemetry unit
74-year-old client receiving IV furosemide.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Complains of muscle weakness and constipation.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• potassium 2.8 mEq/L
• flattened T waves with U waves
• weakness
• urine output increased after diuretic
• appetite fair

title: Acute Kidney Injury From Nephrotoxic Medication

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
66-year-old client receiving IV vancomycin and contrast yesterday.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has decreased urine output and rising creatinine.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• urine output 18 mL/hr
• creatinine doubled from baseline
• vancomycin trough above therapeutic range
• mild nausea
• blood pressure 132/70 mm Hg

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Outpatient dialysis center
57-year-old client with end-stage kidney disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Arrives for hemodialysis and reports chills and tenderness at fistula site.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• temperature 101.6 F
• red warmth over AV fistula
• purulent drainage at access area
• bruit and thrill present
• reports mild fatigue after dialysis

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Home dialysis clinic
60-year-old client performing peritoneal dialysis at home.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Calls reporting abdominal pain and cloudy dialysate outflow.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• cloudy peritoneal drainage
• diffuse abdominal tenderness
• fever 101.2 F
• exit site dressing intact
• drain time slightly longer than usual

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
20-year-old client with sickle cell disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports severe bilateral leg and back pain after dehydration at a soccer tournament.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• pain 9/10 in long bones
• SpO2 90% on room air
• reticulocyte count elevated
• temperature 99.0 F
• reports thirst

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Oncology clinic
47-year-old client receiving chemotherapy for breast cancer.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Calls to report chills and oral temperature 100.8 F.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• absolute neutrophil count 350/mm3
• temperature 100.8 F
• new sore throat
• hair loss from chemotherapy
• mild fatigue

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
55-year-old client with immune thrombocytopenia.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports gum bleeding after brushing teeth.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• platelet count 18,000/mm3
• petechiae on lower legs
• oozing from venipuncture site
• hemoglobin 12.8 g/dL
• appetite intact

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical-surgical unit
43-year-old client receiving first unit of packed red blood cells.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Ten minutes after transfusion starts, client reports chills and low back pain.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• fever and chills during transfusion
• low back pain
• dark urine with hypotension
• IV site patent
• mild anxiety before transfusion

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Rehabilitation unit
68-year-old client recovering from stroke.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has new unilateral calf swelling and tenderness.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• right calf 4 cm larger than left
• warm tender calf
• recent immobility
• pedal pulse palpable
• skin dry

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
73-year-old client with atrial fibrillation.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports sudden severe pain and numbness in left foot.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• pulseless left foot
• pale cool limb
• paresthesia with severe pain
• right foot warm
• temperature 98.2 F

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
29-year-old client with tibia fracture in a splint.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports increasing pain despite prescribed analgesic.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• pain out of proportion
• pain with passive stretch
• tense swollen compartment with paresthesia
• capillary refill 2 seconds initially
• cast edges padded

title: Neurogenic Shock After Spinal Cord Injury

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
35-year-old client after diving accident.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has suspected cervical spinal cord injury.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• hypotension with bradycardia
• warm dry skin below injury
• loss of motor/sensation below shoulders
• facial abrasions
• anxious but oriented

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Rehabilitation unit
42-year-old client with T4 spinal cord injury.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports pounding headache and flushed face during bladder training.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood pressure 210/108 mm Hg
• pounding headache
• distended bladder
• legs are cool below injury baseline
• urine in catheter tubing absent

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Neuro unit
46-year-old client after traumatic brain injury.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Was alert earlier but now is difficult to arouse.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• declining level of consciousness
• new unequal pupils
• systolic hypertension with bradycardia
• scalp dressing dry
• urine output 40 mL/hr

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
70-year-old client with sudden right-sided weakness.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Last known well 45 minutes ago while eating breakfast.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• facial droop and right arm drift
• slurred speech
• symptom onset within thrombolytic window
• blood glucose 104 mg/dL
• blood pressure 178/92 mm Hg

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
60-year-old client taking warfarin.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops sudden severe headache, vomiting, and left-sided weakness.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• worst headache of life
• vomiting with decreased LOC
• INR 5.8
• blood glucose 118 mg/dL
• afebrile

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
31-year-old client with known seizure disorder.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has continuous tonic-clonic activity lasting longer than 5 minutes.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• seizure lasting over 5 minutes
• cyanosis around lips
• no return to baseline between seizures
• old tongue scar
• family brought medication list

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
23-year-old college student.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports fever, severe headache, stiff neck, and photophobia.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• nuchal rigidity
• fever 103.0 F
• petechial rash
• roommate has sore throat
• oriented to name only

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: ICU
50-year-old client with myasthenia gravis.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports increasing weakness after respiratory infection.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• weak cough
• difficulty swallowing secretions
• vital capacity decreased
• ptosis worse late in day
• reports fatigue

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
41-year-old client recovering from viral illness.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports ascending weakness and tingling in feet.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• ascending bilateral weakness
• decreased deep tendon reflexes
• forced vital capacity trending down
• low back discomfort
• afebrile

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
36-year-old client with untreated Graves disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Arrives with fever, agitation, and palpitations after infection.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• temperature 104.2 F
• heart rate 158/min
• severe agitation with diarrhea
• exophthalmos
• warm moist skin

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
82-year-old client with history of hypothyroidism.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Found lethargic at home during cold weather.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• hypothermia
• bradycardia with hypotension
• hypoventilation and decreased LOC
• dry coarse skin
• constipation history

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
40-year-old client with Addison disease.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has vomiting and weakness after stopping prednisone abruptly.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood pressure 78/44 mm Hg
• hyperkalemia with hyponatremia
• severe weakness and abdominal pain
• hyperpigmented skin
• reports nausea

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Postoperative unit
55-year-old client after unilateral adrenalectomy.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Four hours postop, client becomes dizzy and hypotensive.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood pressure 82/46 mm Hg
• serum glucose 56 mg/dL
• severe weakness
• incision dressing dry
• reports incisional pain 3/10

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
28-year-old client with flank pain.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports fever, chills, dysuria, and nausea.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• costovertebral angle tenderness
• temperature 102.9 F
• tachycardia with leukocytosis
• urine cloudy
• reports poor fluid intake

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Postoperative unit
73-year-old client after transurethral resection of prostate.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has continuous bladder irrigation infusing.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• suprapubic distention
• decreased catheter output despite irrigation inflow
• severe bladder spasms
• light pink drainage earlier
• vital signs stable

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Surgical unit
60-year-old client 3 days after abdominal surgery.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports a popping sensation after coughing.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• bowel visible through incision
• sudden serosanguineous drainage
• incision edges separated
• pain 4/10 before event
• temperature 99.0 F

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
34-year-old client with partial- and full-thickness burns after house fire.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Burns involve anterior torso and both arms.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• tachycardia with hypotension
• urine output 15 mL/hr
• deep burns over large body surface area
• singed arm hair only
• reports severe pain

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
44-year-old client rescued from enclosed-space fire.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has facial burns and cough.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• sooty sputum
• hoarse voice
• stridor with facial burns
• singed eyebrows
• anxiety

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
30-year-old client receiving first dose of IV ceftriaxone.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops itching and throat tightness during infusion.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• wheezing and stridor
• hives with facial swelling
• blood pressure 78/40 mm Hg
• IV site patent
• mild nausea

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
27-year-old client found unresponsive with empty oxycodone bottle nearby.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Respirations are slow and shallow.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• respiratory rate 6/min
• pinpoint pupils

• blood glucose 94 mg/dL
• skin cool

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
50-year-old client admitted for pancreatitis.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Last alcohol use was 36 hours ago; now tremulous and diaphoretic.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• visual hallucinations
• blood pressure 178/96 mm Hg with tachycardia

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
46-year-old client with bipolar disorder taking lithium.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports vomiting and diarrhea after taking ibuprofen for back pain.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• coarse tremor
• ataxia
• lithium level 2.1 mEq/L

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
32-year-old client taking sertraline.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Started linezolid yesterday and now has fever and agitation.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• hyperreflexia and clonus

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Psychiatric inpatient unit
28-year-old client started on haloperidol 3 days ago.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops fever and severe muscle rigidity.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• lead-pipe rigidity
• temperature 104.0 F

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Operating room/PACU
21-year-old client under general anesthesia.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: During surgery develops sudden hypercarbia and rigidity.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• rapid rise in end-tidal CO2

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
83-year-old client with heart failure receiving packed RBCs.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Near the end of transfusion, client reports dyspnea.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• crackles and dyspnea during transfusion
• distended neck veins
• blood pressure rises
• temperature 98.9 F
• no rash or back pain

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: ICU
59-year-old client with central venous catheter for vasopressors.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops fever and chills with no clear source.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• temperature 102.4 F
• purulent drainage at central line insertion site

• white dressing date label present
• glucose 140 mg/dL

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical-surgical unit
56-year-old client receiving total parenteral nutrition through central line.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports thirst and frequent urination.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• blood glucose 328 mg/dL
• TPN infusing at prescribed rate

• central line dressing intact
• bowel sounds present

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Long-term acute care
68-year-old client with stroke and gastrostomy tube.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Receives continuous enteral feeding.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• coughing during feeding
• new crackles
• head of bed found flat
• gastric residual within facility parameter
• abdomen soft

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
71-year-old client who received clindamycin.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Has watery diarrhea and abdominal cramping.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• watery foul-smelling diarrhea
• recent antibiotic exposure
• white blood cells elevated
• normal urine output
• mild appetite loss

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Medical unit
62-year-old client with cirrhosis.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Family reports increasing confusion and sleepiness.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• asterixis
• ammonia elevated

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Emergency department
54-year-old client with cirrhosis and portal hypertension.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Vomits a large amount of bright red blood.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Outpatient infusion center
49-year-old client receiving vesicant chemotherapy through peripheral IV.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Reports burning at IV site.

tabs.label: Initial Data
tabs.body:
• burning pain at infusion site

tabs.label: Client Profile
tabs.body:
Setting: Oncology unit
35-year-old client with leukemia beginning induction chemotherapy.

tabs.label: Nurses' Notes
tabs.body: Develops nausea, weakness, and decreased urine output.

tabs.label: Initial Data

• rescue inhaler not immediately available
• no written action plan on file
• speaks full sentences
• SpO2 95%
